title: Panelboard and Breaker Trainer
summary: A service-panel simulator lets learners inspect busbars, breaker positions, neutral and bonding bars, and common protective devices while tracing how branch circuits are fed and protected.
learningObjectives: Identify the major internal parts of a panelboard.
Match breaker ratings and protection types to common branch-circuit applications.
Understand how overcurrent and fault protection isolate unsafe conditions.

environment.setting: Open panelboard with removable dead front, labeled branch circuits, and diagnostic status lights

interactables.label: Main disconnect
interactables.teaches: Shows how the service panel is isolated before internal work begins.

interactables.label: Branch breakers
interactables.teaches: Compares standard, GFCI, and AFCI breaker behavior.

interactables.label: Busbar cutaway
interactables.teaches: Explains how power is distributed across panel spaces.

interactables.label: Trip and reset scenario
interactables.teaches: Shows overload and fault response plus safe reset practice.

tasks: Identify the main disconnect, busbars, neutral bar, and bonding point.
Assign a correct breaker type to each branch-circuit scenario.
Trigger an overload and explain why the selected breaker trips.
Reset the panel in a safe sequence after the fault is cleared.
completion: Learner identifies core panel components accurately.
Learner selects proper protection for each circuit.
Learner completes the trip-and-reset sequence safely.
